content.problemStatement: Identify and correct the error in the following code
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer number.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the number of letters in the input number when written in English.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The number 19 has 8 letters: one nine.
The number 123 has 24 letters: one hundred and twenty three.
The number 456 has 23 letters: four hundred and fifty six.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using a recursive approach to convert the number into words and then counting the number of letters. We can use arrays to store the words for ones, teens, and tens.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log N) per test case, O(T log N) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the space used does not grow with the size of the input.
